$comment_status: PENDING — added per Architecture_PronounAuthor_prompt_and_granularity.md (v1). Needed for items where a clitic-cluster markpoint splits off the verb form as a separate markpoint and the verb is accepted in either present or future. Per the architect's hint, this is the union of verb_form.present_indicative.dare and verb_form.future_simple.dare for the bucket attribution.
proposed_label: dare: present indicative or future simple (either accepted)
proposed_description: A union bucket for items where both 'dai/dà/danno' (present) and 'darai/darà/daranno' (future) are accepted as the correct verb form, because the question is testing pronoun cluster placement rather than tense choice. Existing per-verb-per-tense buckets handle the singular cases; this leaf is for the disjunction.

proposed_attributes.canonical_example: Me lo dai? / Me lo darai?

rationale: Used in op_comb_glielo_10 and op_comb_order_01 after splitting per AUTHOR_BRIEF §2 criterion 8. The architect hinted at this shape; opening a thread to ratify or rename.

$comment_status: RESOLVED 2026-05-14 — see REPLY_TO_pronoun_chat_decisions.md (D3). The sibling bucket pronoun.ne.pronominal_idiomatic_verbs now exists in data/buckets/pronoun.json (lines 728-739) and items op_ne_motion_02 (fregarsene) and op_comb_mene_05 (starsene) have been rebucketed accordingly. op_comb_mene_06 (venirsene) stays in motion_andarsene because venirsene IS motion. Entry retained here for traceability.
proposed_label: Pronominal idiomatic verbs with ne (starsene, fregarsene, intendersene, uscirsene)
proposed_description: A class of lexicalised pronominal verbs where ne is part of the verb's lexical identity in a non-motion idiom. Distinct from motion_andarsene because the ne isn't about movement.

proposed_attributes.canonical_example: Me ne sto a casa. Non me ne frega niente.

rationale: Ratified as a sibling rather than a rename. motion_andarsene now strictly for motion verbs (andarsene, venirsene); pronominal_idiomatic_verbs covers the non-motion family.

$comment_status: RESOLVED AND MIGRATED — sub-leaf exists in data/buckets/pronoun.json (lines 741-751). Migrated 2026-05-15: op_iop_verbs_iop_05 (the only PP piacere-family grammar item) now attributes to this sub-leaf. trans_op_en_it_07 (present tense) and trans_op_en_it_08 (imperfect) don't qualify because the new bucket is specifically for PP behaviour; they stay on the parent.
proposed_label: Piacere-family in passato prossimo: aux essere, participle agrees with grammatical subject
proposed_description: Sub-leaf for the passato-prossimo behaviour of piacere/mancare/sembrare/bastare. Aux essere; participle agrees with the grammatical subject (the thing liked / missed), not with the IOP experiencer.

proposed_attributes.canonical_example: A Marco è piaciuta la festa. Le sono mancate le sue amiche.
proposed_attributes.common_miss: Le ha piaciuto la festa (using avere)

rationale: Ratified. Migration of existing items into this leaf is a follow-up task for the next dispatch.

$comment_status: RESOLVED AND PARTIALLY MIGRATED — sub-leaf exists in data/buckets/pronoun.json (lines 753-763). Migrated 2026-05-15: op_spec_dislo_01 (La pizza, l'ho mangiata) and op_spec_dislo_04 (I biglietti, me li ha dati) now attribute to this sub-leaf — both are the canonical fronted-DO + clitic + PP-agreement pattern. op_spec_dislo_02 stays on the parent (IOP dislocation, no PP agreement applicable). op_spec_dislo_03 stays on the parent (cluster choice tested, not agreement). trans_op_it_en_03 stays on the parent (IOP dislocation). trans_op_it_en_17 stays on the parent (right-dislocation, not left-dislocation).
proposed_label: Left-dislocation with participle agreement on resumed DOP
proposed_description: Specifically the construction where a fronted DO is resumed by a clitic AND the participle in passato prossimo agrees with that fronted DO. Cross-cuts the pronoun tree and the passato_prossimo agreement bucket.

proposed_attributes.canonical_example: La pizza, l'ho mangiata tutta. Le foto, le ho viste tutte.

rationale: Ratified. Migration is a follow-up task.

$comment_status: RESOLVED AND MIGRATED — sub-leaf exists in data/buckets/pronoun.json. Migrated 2026-05-15: op_cil_idiom_03 (the farcela 1sg item) and trans_op_en_it_18 (the I-can't-manage farcela translation) now attribute to this sub-leaf. op_cil_idiom_01 (volerci), op_cil_idiom_02 (metterci), op_cil_idiom_04 (tenerci) stay on the parent because they're different idiomatic verbs.
proposed_label: farcela (with fixed ci + la, vowel change to ce la)
proposed_description: Sub-leaf for farcela specifically. The fixed clitic pattern is ci + la → ce la (vowel change of ci), with la lexicalised.

proposed_attributes.canonical_example: Non ce la faccio. Ce l'ho fatta!
proposed_attributes.common_miss: Non ci la faccio (no vowel change) / Non ce lo faccio (wrong DO clitic)

rationale: Ratified. Migration is a follow-up task.

$comment_status: PENDING — added per FEEDBACK_HOUSE_STYLE_pronoun_chat.md §2. Five items (op_comb_joined_01..05) already author against this proposed bucket. They will be runtime-warned but accepted until promotion.
proposed_label: Joining a pre-verb clitic cluster into one word
proposed_description: Pre-verb clitic clusters (me lo, te ne, ce la, etc.) stay as two written words. The joined form (melo, tene, cela) is what learners write when over-generalising from the post-verb attached pattern (vedermelo, dammene). Distinct from the choice of cluster (which combined.glielo_family etc. handle) and from the IOP/DOP ordering (combined.order_iop_before_dop). The glielo-family clitics (glielo/gliela/glieli/gliele/gliene) are correctly one word and are not in scope.

proposed_attributes.common_miss: melo, telo, celo, velo, mela, tela, cela, vela, mene, tene, cene, vene

rationale: Real-world miss observed on op_dop_dislocation_01 ('melo' written for 'me l''). Currently the tree treats this as 'didn't try' rather than as a specific known confusion. A dedicated bucket lets the marker show 'you joined the cluster' as the diagnostic, and lets the platform track this learner pattern across many questions.
